channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Microsoft Exchange Zero-Day Under Attack, No Patch Available
item.description: CVE-2026-42897 stems from a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability and can allow an attacker to compromise Outlook Web Access (OWA) mailboxes.

item.title: SHub macOS infostealer variant spoofs Apple security updates
item.description: A new variant of the 'SHub' macOS infostealer uses AppleScript to show a fake security update message and installs a backdoor. [...]

item.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
item.description: The now patched vulnerabilities in the rapidly growing AI agent framework allow attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence.

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and...

item.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-05-17, (Mon, May 18th)
item.description: Since the last update, the TeamPCP supply chain campaign produced its loudest stretch since the March Trivy disclosure: an officially confirmed Checkmarx Jenkins plugin compromise and a new self-spreading Mini Shai-Hu...

item.title: Shai-Hulud Worm Clones Spread After Code Release
item.description: The release of Shai-Hulud source code spells trouble for software developers as researchers worry the self-replicating worm could scale.

item.title: Vulnerability Summary for the Week of May 11, 2026
item.description: High Vulnerabilities Primary Vendor -- Product Description Published CVSS Score Source Info Patch Info acl--ACL Analytics ACL Analytics versions 11.x through 13.0.0.579 contain an arbitrary code execution vulnerabilit...

item.title: Fuel Tank Breaches Expand Scope of Iran's Cyber Offensive
item.description: Security experts have long warned that insecure automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems exposed on the Internet can be tampered with by threat actors.

item.title: ShinyHunters hack 7-Eleven: franchisee data and Salesforce records exposed
item.description: 7-Eleven confirmed a breach after ShinyHunters claimed theft of over 600,000 Salesforce records and franchisee data. 7-Eleven has confirmed a data breach after the ShinyHunters hacking group claimed it stole more than...

item.title: Grafana says stolen GitHub token let hackers steal codebase
item.description: Grafana Labs disclosed that hackers have downloaded its source code after breaching its GitHub environment using a stolen access token. [...]

item.title: Attackers are exploiting critical NGINX vulnerability (CVE-2026-42945)
item.description: A critical NGINX vulnerability (CVE-2026-42945) disclosed last week is being exploited by attackers, VulnCheck security researcher Patrick Garrity revealed on Saturday. The vulnerability, dubbed NGINX Rift, can be rel...

item.title: Boulevard of Broken Dreams: 2 Decades of Cyber Fails
item.description: From the MGM and Caesars fiasco and MOVEit's patch nightmare to epic business blunders and the jaded reality of living in a post-breach world, Dark Reading looks back at the mistakes, miscalculations, systemic failure...

item.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, n8n Patch RCE, SQL Injection, Privilege Escalation Flaws
item.description: Ivanti, Fortinet, n8n, SAP, and VMware have released security fixes for various vulnerabilities that could be exploited by bad actors to bypass authentication and execute arbitrary code. Topping the list is a critical...

item.title: Grafana Confirms Breach After Hackers Claim They Stole Data
item.description: Grafana appears to have been targeted by Coinbase Cartel, a cybercrime group linked to ShinyHunters, Scattered Spider, and Lapsus$. The post Grafana Confirms Breach After Hackers Claim They Stole Data appeared first o...

item.title: Microsoft confirms Windows 11 security update install issues
item.description: Microsoft has confirmed that the May 2026 Windows 11 security update (KB5089549) fails to install on some systems and triggers 0x800f0922 errors. [...]

item.title: Exploit available for new DirtyDecrypt Linux root escalation flaw
item.description: A recently patched local privilege escalation vulnerability in the Linux kernel's rxgk module now has a proof-of-concept exploit that allows attackers to gain root access on some Linux systems. [...]

item.title: Hackers earn $1,298,250 for 47 zero-days at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026
item.description: The Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 hacking contest has concluded, with security researchers collecting $1,298,250 in rewards after exploiting 47 zero-day flaws. [...]

item.title: 5 Steps to Managing Shadow AI Tools Without Slowing Down Employees
item.description: Many employees already use shadow AI tools at work without security review. Adaptive Security breaks down how teams can build practical AI governance without adding friction for employees. [...]

item.title: AI is drowning software maintainers in junk security reports
item.description: AI-assisted vulnerability research has exploded, unleashing a firehose of low-quality reports on overworked software maintainers who are wasting hours sifting through noise instead of fixing real problems. Linus Torva...

item.title: Leaked Shai-Hulud malware fuels new npm infostealer campaign
item.description: The Shai-Hulud malware leaked last week is now used in new attacks on the Node Package Manager (npm) index, as infected packages emerged over the weekend. [...]

item.title: Game over for 74 suspected scammers after Dutch cops plastered their faces on billboards
item.description: The Dutch police’s Game Over?! campaign, which publicly displays images of suspected fraudsters to encourage self-surrenders and gather public tips, is proving successful, with the identities of 74 of the 100 suspects...

item.title: GenAI Risks in Cloud Environments: What Security Teams Are Actually Missing in 2026
item.description: Key Takeaways Most cloud security programs were not built for generative AI workloads. The IAM policies, network segmentation rules, and misconfiguration checks that worked for a three-tier web application in 2021 lea...

item.title: What Is Multi-Cloud Security?
item.description: Multi-cloud security protects workloads, identities, and data across multiple public cloud environments while maintaining consistent visibility and security controls. As organizations adopt multi-cloud strategies to i...

item.title: What Is Cloud Detection and Response (CDR)?
item.description: Cloud environments move fast, and attackers know it. Traditional security tools built for static infrastructure often fail to detect the complex, multi-stage threats that unfold across cloud workloads, identities, API...

item.title: TanStack weighs invitation-only pull requests after supply chain attack
item.description: Shai-Hulud worm exploited GitHub Actions misconfiguration to poison shared cache, now project weighing nuclear option on unsolicited contributions

item.title: San Francisco Secure Software and AppSec Summit 2026: The Next AppSec Operating Model
item.description: Security leaders at this SF area Summit examined AI agent risk, dependency governance, stale infrastructure, and the future of secure software.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Exchange 0-Day, npm Worm, Fake AI Repo, Cisco Exploit and More
item.description: Monday opens with a trust problem. A mail server flaw is under active use. A network control system was targeted. Trusted packages were poisoned. A fake model page pushed a stealer. Then came the familiar ransom claim...

item.title: The Infosecurity Europe Cyber Startup Competition: Meet the Finalists
item.description: New for 2026, the Infosecurity Europe Startup competition will see five finalists pitch their ideas in front of a live audience, including senior industry leaders, investors and buyers

item.title: Public Amazon bucket leaks sensitive guest data from Japanese hotel platform Tabiq
item.description: A hotel check-in system exposed over 1 million passports, IDs, and selfies online due to a misconfigured cloud storage bucket. A security lapse in the Reqrea’s Tabiq hotel check-in system exposed over 1 million passpo...

item.title: NGINX Rift attackers waste no time targeting exposed servers
item.description: Researchers say 18-year-old flaw already being probed and exploited just days after disclosure

item.title: How to Reduce Phishing Exposure Before It Turns into Business Disruption
item.description: What happens when a phishing email looks clean enough to pass through security, but dangerous enough to expose the business after one click? That is the gap many SOCs still struggle with: the attacks that leave teams...

item.title: Millions Impacted Across Several US Healthcare Data Breaches
item.description: Several healthcare data breaches impacting hundreds of thousands and even millions were added to the HHS tracker. The post Millions Impacted Across Several US Healthcare Data Breaches appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: SmartBear expands ReadyAPI with AI-powered API testing capabilities
item.description: SmartBear has announced ReadyAPI’s new AI test generation capability that accelerates API testing by up to 80% while giving teams control to enable or disable AI. While competitors focus on speed alone, ReadyAPI’s AI...

item.title: Poland directs officials to ditch Signal in favor of 'secure' state-developed alternative
item.description: Shift comes amid mounting reports of successful social engineering attacks targeting higher-ups in government

item.title: Developer Workstations Are Now Part of the Software Supply Chain
item.description: Supply chain attackers are not only trying to slip malicious code into trusted software. They are trying to steal the access that makes trusted software possible. Recently, three separate campaigns hit npm, PyPI, and...

item.title: Zero-Day Exploit Against Windows BitLocker
item.description: It’s nasty , but it requires physical access to the computer: The exploit, named YellowKey, was published earlier this week by a researcher who goes by the alias Nightmare-Eclipse. It reliably bypasses default Windows...

item.title: From Cryptographic Blind Spots to Post-Quantum Agility: Introducing Wiz for PQC Readiness
item.description: Eliminate cryptographic blind spots and neutralize legacy debt with an integrated cryptographic asset inventory. Identify risks across code, cloud, and runtime, using the Wiz Security Graph to prioritize migration and...

item.title: Researcher Drops MiniPlasma Windows Exploit for Unpatched 2020 CVE
item.description: The researcher dropped the MiniPlasma exploit that uses the original proof-of-concept (PoC) code targeting the bug. The post Researcher Drops MiniPlasma Windows Exploit for Unpatched 2020 CVE appeared first on Securit...

item.title: NCSC Publishes Guidance on Securing Agentic AI Use
item.description: The UK’s National Cyber Security Centre is helping organizations to understand agentic AI security risks

item.title: Four Malicious npm Packages Deliver Infostealers and Phantom Bot DDoS Malware
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered four new npm packages containing information-stealing malware, one of which is a clone of the Shai-Hulud worm open-sourced by TeamPCP. The list of identified packages is below...

item.title: Project Glasswing: what Mythos showed us
item.description: In recent weeks, we pointed Mythos and other security-focused LLMs at live code across critical parts of our infrastructure. We share what we observed, the models’ strengths and weaknesses, and what the work around th...

item.title: Mozilla warns UK: Breaking VPNs will not magically fix Britain's age-check mess
item.description: Firefox maker says the tools are basic security infrastructure, not teenage contraband

item.title: Bank of England, FCA and Treasury Raise Alarm Over Frontier AI
item.description: The UK’s financial authorities have set expectations for the sector on cybersecurity and operational resilience

item.title: 201 arrested in INTERPOL disruption of phishing and fraud networks
item.description: Operation Ramz, a cybercrime initiative coordinated by INTERPOL across the MENA region, focused on disrupting phishing campaigns, malware activity, and cyber scams that caused substantial financial losses across the r...

item.title: Exploitation of Critical NGINX Vulnerability Begins
item.description: The flaw leads to denial-of-service on default configurations and to remote code execution if ASLR is disabled. The post Exploitation of Critical NGINX Vulnerability Begins appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Pre-Stuxnet Fast16 Malware Tampered with Nuclear Weapons Simulations
item.description: A new analysis of the Lua-based fast16 malware has confirmed that it was a cyber sabotage tool designed to tamper with nuclear weapons testing simulations. According to Broadcom-owned Symantec and Carbon Black teams,...

item.title: The AI backdoor your security stack is not built to see
item.description: Enterprises deploying LLMs have spent the past two years building defenses around a reasonable assumption: malicious behavior leaves a trace in the input. Scan for suspicious tokens, filter unusual characters, watch f...

item.title: Lyrie: Open-source autonomous pentesting agent
item.description: Penetration testing has usually required weeks of manual work, specialized tooling, and teams with narrow skill sets. Lyrie, an open-source autonomous security agent built by OTT Cybersecurity, compresses that process...

item.title: AI shrinks vulnerability exploitation window to hours
item.description: Time has become organizations’ biggest vulnerability because the gap between vulnerability discovery and exploitation has narrowed to hours, according to Synack’s 2026 State of Vulnerabilities Report. Total vulnerabil...

item.title: Product showcase: McAfee + ChatGPT integration turns doubt into a scam check
item.description: McAfee + ChatGPT integration brings real-time scam detection in conversations and gives users an easier way to verify suspicious content before clicking or responding. It is available to anyone, without requiring a Mc...

item.title: When ransomware hits, confidence doesn’t restore endpoints
item.description: Ransomware, supply chain vulnerabilities, insider threats, compliance failures, and software disruptions remain major concerns for security leaders, according to The Ransomware Reality: Zero Days to Recover report by...

item.title: Can Laws Stop Deepfakes? South Korea Aims to Find Out
item.description: South Korea's local elections next month will be a test bed for how effective regulations might be to stymie the flow of deepfakes.

item.title: The Boring Stuff is Dangerous Now
item.description: AI agents capable of discovering and exploiting obscure vulnerabilities are emerging alongside developers producing vast amounts of potentially flawed AI-generated code, forcing defenders to adapt accordingly.

item.title: How to create custom cloud security controls faster with headless cloud security
item.description: Modern CSPM platforms like Sysdig help organizations identify cloud risks and misconfigurations, but building and operationalizing custom security controls can still be slow and complex. Creating policies often requir...

item.title: Pathfinding Labs: Deploy, test, and learn from 100+ intentionally vulnerable AWS environments
item.description: Introducing Pathfinding Labs, a collection of intentionally vulnerable AWS environments for red teamers and blue teamers to deploy, exploit, and use for detection validation.

item.title: Security Affairs newsletter Round 577 by Pierluigi Paganini – INTERNATIONAL EDITION
item.description: A new round of the weekly Security Affairs newsletter has arrived! Every week, the best security articles from Security Affairs are free in your email box. Enjoy a new round of the weekly SecurityAffairs newsletter, i...

item.title: Linus Torvalds says AI-powered bug hunters have made Linux security mailing list ‘almost entirely unmanageable’
item.description: Multiple researchers using the same tools to find the same bugs are creating ‘unnecessary pain and pointless work’

item.title: Debian 13.5 point release lands with security fixes, bug patches
item.description: Debian 13.5 is the fifth point release for the stable distribution “trixie.” The update folds in roughly 100 Debian Security Advisories and corrections for more than 130 source packages, covering everything from the L...

item.title: Tycoon2FA hijacks Microsoft 365 accounts via device-code phishing
item.description: The Tycoon2FA phishing kit now supports device-code phishing attacks and abuses Trustifi click-tracking URLs to hijack Microsoft 365 accounts. [...]

item.title: Week in review: Cisco patches SD-WAN 0-day, unpatched Microsoft Exchange Server flaw exploited
item.description: Here’s an overview of some of last week’s most interesting news, articles, interviews and videos: Review: Foundations of Cybersecurity, 2nd edition Jason Andress has refreshed his introductory security text for No Sta...

item.title: Funnel Builder Flaw Under Active Exploitation Enables WooCommerce Checkout Skimming
item.description: A critical security vulnerability impacting the Funnel Builder plugin for WordPress has come under active exploitation in the wild to inject malicious JavaScript code into WooCommerce checkout pages with the goal of s...

item.title: Russian hackers turn Kazuar backdoor into modular P2P botnet
item.description: The Russian hacker group Secret Blizzard has developed its long-running Kazuar backdoor into a modular peer-to-peer (P2P) botnet designed for long-term persistence, stealth, and data collection. [...]

item.title: PoC Code Published for Critical NGINX Vulnerability
item.description: Introduced in 2008, the critical-severity security defect was patched this week in NGINX Plus and NGINX open source. The post PoC Code Published for Critical NGINX Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Bigfin Squid
item.description: Article about the bigfin squid. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: Popular node-ipc npm package compromised to steal credentials
item.description: Hackers have injected credential-stealing malware into newly published versions of node-ipc, a popular inter-process communication package, in a new supply chain attack targeting npm. [...]

item.title: Cisco Patches Another SD-WAN Zero-Day, the Sixth Exploited in 2026
item.description: The zero-day, tracked as CVE-2026-20182, has been exploited in targeted attacks by a sophisticated threat actor identified as UAT-8616. The post Cisco Patches Another SD-WAN Zero-Day, the Sixth Exploited in 2026 appea...

item.title: Avada Builder WordPress plugin flaws allow site credential theft
item.description: Two vulnerabilities in the Avada Builder plugin for WordPress, with an estimated one million active installations, allow hackers to read arbitrary files and extract sensitive information from the database. [...]

item.title: Microsoft Exchange, Windows 11 hacked on second day of Pwn2Own
item.description: ​During the second day of Pwn2Own Berlin 2026, competitors collected $385,750 in cash awards after exploiting 15 unique zero-day vulnerabilities in multiple products, including Windows 11, Microsoft Exchange, and Red...

item.title: Metasploit Wrap-Up 05/15/2026
item.description: Weaponizing a text editor for fun and profit Gather round, dear readers, because today, we (by we, we mean @h00die) dropped the ultimate persistence mechanism: Vim plugin persistence. And honestly, calling it "persist...

item.title: The AWS AI Security Framework: Securing AI with the right controls, at the right layers, at the right phases
item.description: TL;DR for busy executives The AWS AI Security Framework helps security leaders move fast and stay secure with AI. Security compounds from day 1 as workloads evolve from prototype to production to scale. Assess first....

item.title: Linux kernel vulnerability enables local theft of SSH host keys and /etc/shadow
item.description: Executive Summary A high-impact Linux kernel vulnerability, currently without a verified public CVE or CVSS score, was disclosed affecting kernels prior to commit 31e62c2e. The issue allows a local unprivileged attack...

item.title: Welcome to BlackFile: Inside a Vishing Extortion Operation
item.description: Written by: Austin Larsen, Tyler McLellan, Genevieve Stark, Dan Ebreo Introduction Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has continued to track an expansive extortion campaign by UNC6671, a threat actor operating un...

item.title: In Other News: Big Tech vs Canada Encryption Bill, Cisco’s Free AI Security Spec, Audi App Flaws
item.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Nvidia cloud gaming data breach, Android 17 security upgrades, FBI warning after ShinyHunters hacks Canvas. The post In Other News: Big Tech vs Canada...

item.title: Gremlin Stealer Evolves into Modular Threat with Advanced Evasion Capabilities
item.description: A new Gremlin stealer variant has evolved into a modular toolkit with advanced evasion and data theft capabilities, according to new Unit 42 research

item.title: Inside the REMUS Infostealer: Session Theft, MaaS, and Rapid Evolution
item.description: Stolen browser sessions and authentication tokens are becoming more valuable than stolen passwords. Flare explains how the REMUS infostealer evolved around session theft and operational scalability. [...]

item.title: Google lets Workspace admins apply one policy across all SAML apps
item.description: Google has updated Context-Aware Access (CAA) in Google Workspace to introduce a default policy assignment for SAML applications. SAML applications are third-party or internal applications that use the Security Assert...

item.title: What 45 Days of Watching Your Own Tools Will Tell You About Your Real Attack Surface
item.description: In Your Biggest Security Risk Isn't Malware - It's What You Already Trust, we made a simple argument: the most dangerous activity inside most organizations no longer looks like an attack. It looks like administration....

item.title: Akamai to acquire LayerX for $205 million
item.description: Akamai has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire LayerX, a provider of browser-based AI usage control and secure enterprise browser (SEB) technology. LayerX’s solutions will extend Akamai’s protection into th...

item.title: American Lending Center Data Breach Affects 123,000 Individuals
item.description: The non-bank lender discovered a ransomware attack nearly one year ago, but only recently completed its investigation. The post American Lending Center Data Breach Affects 123,000 Individuals appeared first on Securit...

item.title: Bypassing On-Camera Age-Verification Checks
item.description: Some AI-based video age-verification checks can be fooled with a fake mustache .

item.title: Thieves unlock stolen iPhones using cheap tools sold on Telegram
item.description: Helping a friend recover a stolen phone, Infoblox researchers uncovered a thriving Telegram-based underground marketplace selling unlocking tools and phishing infrastructure used to monetize stolen iPhones. Activation...

item.title: Ghostwriter group resumes attacks on Ukrainian Government targets
item.description: ESET uncovered new Ghostwriter (aka FrostyNeighbor) activity targeting Ukrainian government organizations in a campaign active since March 2026. ESET researchers published a new report documenting fresh activity attri...

item.title: Rocky Linux launches opt-in security repository for urgent fixes
item.description: Rocky Linux has introduced a Security Repository that allows the distribution to ship urgent security fixes ahead of upstream Enterprise Linux when public exploit code exists and upstream patches are unavailable. “The...

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Deploy New TencShell Malware Against Global Manufacturer
item.description: A suspected China-linked threat actor targeted the Indian branch of a global manufacturer leveraging an open source offensive toolkit

item.title: Chrome 148 Update Patches Critical Vulnerabilities
item.description: The refresh resolves critical-severity use-after-free and other types of bugs in various browser components. The post Chrome 148 Update Patches Critical Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Keycard helps developers secure autonomous AI agents with scoped access
item.description: Keycard has announced Keycard for Multi-Agent Apps, extending its platform to support delegated, session-based access across systems of autonomous agents. Keycard lets developers build apps where every agent has its o...

item.title: [Guest Diary] New Malware Libraries means New Signatures, (Fri, May 15th)
Deepfake detection is losing ground to generative models
item.description: Deepfake detection has been built around a single question for close to a decade. Given a video or audio clip, is it real or synthetic? Commercial detectors analyze pixels, frequencies, and biometric signals to answer...

item.title: Zombie linkages are keeping expired domains trusted for years
item.description: Domains expire, get transferred, and return to the market every day. The systems connected to those domains can continue trusting the original owner long after control has changed. Researchers at USC and the Universit...

item.title: The AI oversight paradox: Is the investment worth the cost of watching it?
item.description: Unlike in 2025, when AI adoption and testing drove business strategies, organizations in 2026 want proven ROI before committing budgets, according to a report by Globalization Partners. How global executives character...

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 15th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9934, (Fri, May 15th)
New infosec products of the week: May 15, 2026
item.description: Here’s a look at the most interesting products from the past week Alation, Apricorn, Versa Networks, and TrustCloud. The questionnaire-based TPRM model is broken, and TrustCloud has a fix TrustCloud announced a new ve...

item.title: Taiwan Incident Highlights Cybersecurity Gaps in Rail Systems
item.description: A Taiwanese student experimenting with software-defined radio technology shut down three bullet trains for nearly an hour, leading to an anti-terrorism response.

item.title: Falco turns 10. Congratulations from Sysdig!
item.description: Back when we announced Falco, we couldn’t imagine its impact. Ten years later, the Falco project has evolved into a thriving community supporting a full ecosystem for cloud-native security.

item.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Auth Bypass Actively Exploited to Gain Admin Access
item.description: Cisco has released updates to address a maximum-severity authentication bypass flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller that it said has been exploited in limited attacks. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20182, carri...

item.title: PraisonAI CVE-2026-44338 Auth Bypass Targeted Within Hours of Disclosure
item.description: Threat actors have been observed attempting to exploit a recently disclosed security vulnerability in PraisonAI, an open-source multi-agent orchestration framework, within four hours of its public disclosure. The vuln...

item.title: Most Organizations Now Use AI Agents for Sensitive Security Tasks
item.description: Semperis study finds 74% of organizations believe AI will increase attacks on identity infrastructure

item.title: ICO Publishes Five-Step Plan to Counter Emerging AI-Powered Attacks
item.description: The Information Commissioner’s Office has released new guidance on how to mitigate the risk of AI-powered attacks

item.title: AI models are getting better at replacing cybersecurity pros on certain tasks
item.description: UK researchers find LLMs are learning to finish jobs faster and improving all the time

item.title: 18-Year-Old NGINX Rewrite Module Flaw Enables Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed multiple security vulnerabilities impacting NGINX Plus and NGINX Open, including a critical flaw that remained undetected for 18 years. The vulnerability, discovered by depthfi...

item.title: Foxconn Attack Highlights Manufacturing's Cyber Crisis
item.description: A Nitrogen ransomware attack on Foxconn's North American facilities is one of 600 hits on manufacturers this year, as gangs increasingly target the sector for its low tolerance for downtime.

item.title: Congress Puts Heat on Instructure After Canvas Outage
item.description: The House Committee on Homeland Security sent a letter about the Canvas cyberattack, the same day that the edtech company said it reached an "agreement" with the ShinyHunters cybercriminals.

item.title: Now Live: The CrowdStrike 2026 Financial Services Threat Landscape Report
AI cyber capability is speeding past earlier projections
item.description: AI cyber capability is improving faster than expected, with newer models surpassing earlier projections, according to the UK government’s AI Security Institute (AISI). AISI measures AI cyber capability using “time hor...

item.title: TeamPCP hackers advertise Mistral AI code repos for sale
item.description: The TeamPCP hacker group is threatening to leak source code from the Mistral AI project unless a buyer is found for the data. [...]

item.title: Hackers exploit auth bypass flaw in Burst Statistics WordPress plugin
item.description: Hackers are leveraging a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in the WordPress plugin Burst Statistics to obtain admin-level access to websites. [...]

item.title: SecurityScorecard Snags Driftnet to Level Up Threat Intelligence
item.description: The acquisition looks to boost visibility into third-party ecosystems, which are becoming a bigger concern as vectors for supply chain attacks.

item.title: Regional routing for AWS access portals: Implementing custom vanity domains for IAM Identity Center
item.description: AWS IAM Identity Center provides a web-based access portal that gives your workforce a single place to view their AWS accounts and applications. With the recent launch of IAM Identity Center multi-Region replication,...

item.title: Maximum Severity Cisco SD-WAN Bug Exploited in the Wild
item.description: This is the second time this year a threat actor has leveraged a CVSS 10.0 vulnerability in Cisco's network control system.

item.title: CVE-2026-0265: Authentication Bypass in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS
item.description: Overview On May 13, 2026, Palo Alto Networks published a security advisory for CVE-2026-0265 , a signature verification vulnerability that facilitates authentication bypass on PAN-OS , the operating system that most P...

item.title: The time of much patching is coming
item.description: In this week’s newsletter, Martin reflects on what the next iteration of AI tools means for vulnerability discovery and our ability to manage large-scale patch releases.

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: PAN-OS RCE, Mythos cURL Bug, AI Tokenizer Attacks, and 10+ Stories
item.description: Everything is still on fire. This week feels dumb in the worst way - bad links, weak checks, fake help desks, shady forum posts, and people turning supply chain attacks into some cursed little game for clout and cash....

item.title: 'FrostyNeighbor' APT Carefully Targets Govt Orgs in Poland, Ukraine
item.description: Attackers uniquely fingerprint victims before delivering spear-phishing payloads aimed at espionage, in the latest campaign from the Belarussian nation-state threat group.

item.title: Automating post-quantum cryptography readiness using AWS Config
item.description: Migrating your TLS endpoints to Post-quantum cryptography (PQC) starts with understanding your current TLS endpoint inventory and posture. This post introduces the PQC Readiness Scanner - an automated tool that invent...

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.description: This is a current list of where and when I am scheduled to speak: I’m giving a virtual talk on “The Security of Trust in the Age of AI,” hosted by the Financial Women’s Association of New York , at 6:00 PM ET on May 2...

item.title: The Dark Side of Efficiency: When Network Controllers Become "God Mode" for Attackers
item.description: Imagine you build a massive corporate campus with every security control money can buy. Blast resistant doors. Biometric scanners. Guards at every entrance. Maybe something similar to the infamous Death Star. On paper...

item.title: Cyber-Enabled Cargo Crime: How Cybercrime Tradecraft is Used to Steal Freight
item.description: Cargo theft now starts with phishing emails and stolen credentials, not hijackings, to reroute and steal freight from supply chains. NMFTA outlines how cyber-enabled cargo crime is changing transportation security. [...]

item.title: Enhancing Data Center Security Without Sacrificing Performance
item.description: For AI data centers, where the stakes are the highest and performance constraints are the tightest, security and performance are no longer a zero-sum game. The post Enhancing Data Center Security Without Sacrificing P...

item.title: HYCU aiR detects insider risk and AI activity from backups
item.description: HYCU has announced HYCU aiR (AI Resilience), an AI-native solution that turns backup data across dozens of applications into a live and actionable intelligence for security, compliance, and IT teams. aiR lets organiza...

item.title: Google Launches Android Spyware Forensics Tool for High-Risk Users
item.description: Google’s Android Advanced Protection Mode is getting a new feature allowing trusted security experts to investigate potential spyware infections
